🤖 Autonomous AI QA Engine — System Architecture

Monolithic Full-Stack Architecture · Node.js + Playwright + LLM API

🌐 Client Layer
🖥️
User Browser
QA engineer enters target URL and triggers analysis from any browser.
Entry Point
HTTP Request · POST /analyze (URL payload)
JSON Report · Issues + Score
🎨 Frontend Layer
⚛️
React / HTML UI
Single-page interface to input URL and display analysis results.
React or HTML/JS
🎨
Tailwind CSS
Utility-first styling for rapid UI prototyping and responsive layout.
Styling
📊
Results Dashboard
Renders bug list, severity badges, hygiene score & defect details.
Display Layer
Express REST API · POST /analyze
⚙️ Backend Layer — Node.js / Express (Monolith)
🚀
Express.js API Server
Receives /analyze requests, orchestrates the test engine pipeline.
REST API
🎭
Playwright Crawler
Launches headless Chromium/Firefox, crawls pages, clicks flows, captures console errors & network logs.
Browser Automation
🔍
Bug Detection Logic
Detects 404 links, JS console errors, DOM anomalies, broken flows. Builds issue knowledge graph.
QA Engine
📋
Report Compiler
Aggregates defects into structured JSON: severity levels, issue types & overall hygiene score.
Score: 0–100
Raw issues · Classify & Score
Persist logs / reports
🧠 AI / LLM Layer
🤖
OpenAI / LLM API
Classifies defects by severity, suggests self-healing fixes, provides natural-language summaries.
GPT-4 / Claude
🗄️ Data Layer
💾
In-Memory / JSON
MVP default: results stored in memory or flat JSON files. No DB overhead.
MVP Mode
🍃
MongoDB
Optional NoSQL store for persistent logs, crawl history & defect graphs.
Persistent
🚢 Deployment
🐳
Docker
Containerized
☁️
Heroku / AWS
Cloud Hosting
🟢
Node.js 20+
Runtime
🎭
Playwright
Chromium / Firefox
Client Flow
Frontend
Backend Core
AI Layer (Optional)
Storage (Optional)
